description: baseline: BERT-base + SciERC + supervised NER+RE, bs=16, lr=3e-5 (56s on DGX). NER F1 still rising at step 500, not converged. First real-data result after Stage 1 migration.

same as 001 but 2000 steps (219s). NER F1 plateau at ~0.63 by step 1000; peak at step 1400 (NER 0.639, Triple 0.128). RE F1 stuck at 0.25-0.27 throughout. Train loss → 0.02 = clear overfitting after step 1700. Ceiling for BERT-base + naive heads identified.

swap bert-base-uncased → allenai/scibert_scivocab_uncased. 1400 steps (151s). PEAK at step 800 (NER 0.653, Triple 0.136), 600 steps EARLIER than BERT-base. Final at 1400 also competitive. Confirms (a) SciBERT small NER win +0.014, (b) RE F1 still locked at 0.27 — RE head is the real bottleneck, not backbone.

upgraded RE head to span features (concat + ⊙ + |Δ| + width emb, 4H+2W input). NER F1 peak 0.616 (DOWN -0.037 vs 002), RE F1 peak 0.279 (UP +0.009), Triple peak 0.119 (DOWN -0.017). Negative result that exposed a deeper bug: compute_loss never trains the RE head to predict NO_RELATION because we drop unlabeled span pairs from the loss. The RE F1 ceiling is in the loss, not the head architecture. Next: stage2-004 must fix the loss before any further head tuning.

BREAKTHROUGH. Reverted RE head to naive 2H concat (= same as 002), then added NO_REL=8th class to the loss: enumerate ALL ordered gold-span pairs, label unannotated ones as NO_REL, train CE over 8 classes. Eval drops NO_REL predictions before forming triples. Result: NER F1 peak 0.633, RE F1 peak 0.514, Triple F1 peak 0.276 — RE F1 nearly doubled (+0.244 over 002), Triple F1 doubled (+0.140 over 002). RE F1 0.514 already ties SpERT SOTA 0.508 with a much simpler head. Confirms: the entire RE F1 ceiling 0.27 was a loss-function bug, not architecture/backbone.
